threats.title: Database query injection
threats.description: Query injection via user inputs and direct database interaction poses a threat, enabling the extraction of information and the injection of data into the database.
threats.mitigation:
Input validation must be implemented for every entry point, and no user input should be trusted.

A sanitization function must be implemented to convert inputs into strings, thereby preventing any attempts to bypass or manipulate the queries.

threats.title: Verifying user permissions
threats.description: Verify the permissions of each user to prevent tampering, impersonation, or unauthorized privilege escalation.
threats.mitigation: You must manage the JWT lifecycle and, before executing any flow, call a function to verify the user's authorization status.

threats.title: Information leakage between users
threats.description:
Is it possible to view another person's balance?
Is it possible to read and edit another person's name?
threats.mitigation:
Access permissions must be configured so that User A can only see the name of User B but cannot view their balance.
Permissions must be isolated between users.

threats.title: Data leakage during information transfer
threats.description: You must ensure that data entered into the bot is encrypted using HTTPS and TLS 1.2 protocols to prevent the interception of any data between the user and the chatbot API.
threats.mitigation:
Using HTTPS and TLS 2 for data transmission.
Isolate users from one another and assign a session to each user.

threats.title: Leaking information to individuals from the bot.
threats.description: Ensure the bot operates in an isolated environment with no access to user data, preventing any potential leakage resulting from manipulation of the bot.
threats.mitigation:
Isolate it from any information regarding individuals.
Delete the session immediately upon completion to prevent the conversation from being leaked.

threats.title: Sanitizing inputs
threats.description: Are inputs sanitized to prevent SQL injection?
threats.mitigation: You must implement allowlists and blocklists for acceptable input data types, as well as validate the query in the user-fetching function and cast it to a string to prevent unauthorized query execution.

threats.title: Data integrity within databases
threats.description: In the event of a database leak, it is essential to ensure that sensitive data within the database is protected by strong encryption, along with a key to rotate the primary encryption; this ensures that even if the data is leaked, it remains difficult to decrypt.
threats.mitigation:
The password field must be strongly encrypted using a high number of iterations to make decryption difficult in the event of a data leak.

A salt must be included when hashing the password and stored in a secure location.

threats.title: SSRF
threats.description: Unauthorized access via the injection of links pointing to the cloud to extract keys.
threats.mitigation:
التاكد من تعقيم مدخلات جلبب الروابط في المدخلات

فرض قائمه بيضاء

threats.title: Leaking files stored in the cloud
threats.description: You must verify the cloud security settings to prevent unauthorized external access to the files and avoid data leaks.
threats.mitigation:
Configuring optimal settings to prevent data leakage:

Establishing a cloud communication whitelist.

Including an API key for cloud communication; access is denied without it.
